title: Tephrocactus udonis
intro: Tephrocactus udonis, also known as the button prickly pear or button hedgehog cactus, is a species of cactus that belongs to the Euphorbiaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Mexico and the southwestern United States, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and combat infections. The bioactive constituents of Tephrocactus udonis include alkaloids, flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as topical creams and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, eczema, and dermatitis.
taxonomy: Tephrocactus udonis is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It is native to China and is a member of the genus Tephrocactus, which is closely related to Pediocactus. The classification of Tephrocactus udonis is within the subfamily Cactoideae and tribe Pachycereeae. The genus Tephrocactus is further grouped into the family Cactaceae and the order Caryophyllales. Tephrocactus udonis is often classified as a species of small to medium-sized cactus with unique and rounded stems.
